articles.title: Odd Lots: How Franchising Led to the Gig Economy (Podcast)
articles.summary: When the fast food industry began booming in the 1950s, it did so via a new business model known as the franchise. This model allowed independent operators to license trademarks from a business like McDonald’s or Dunkin Donuts, and it soon spread across the country, with huge consequences for how Americans are employed. Legal battles fought by franchises eventually opened the door to what’s now known as the gig economy, allowing Uber drivers to be treated in much the same way as the operator of

articles.title: Iran launches attacks on US army facilities in Bahrain, Jordan
articles.summary: Iran launches attacks on US army facilities in Bahrain, Jordan The Iranian army announced that it targeted US infrastructure in Jordan and Bahrain on Friday morning using drone strikes.  It targeted fuel tanks, warehouses and large equipment depots as well as barracks sheltering US troops at the Sheikh Isa Air Base in Bahrain. Meanwhile, an aircraft hangar and US army barracks were also targeted at the Jordanian al-Azraq Air Base.

articles.title: Malaysia envoy summoned by US over Israel stance
articles.summary: Malaysia envoy summoned by US over Israel stance The US  State Department summoned the Malaysian ambassador to Washington in order to explain Kuala Lumpur's position on Israel, state news agency Bernama reported. Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan said Ambassador Muhammad Shahrul Ikram Yaakob told the State Department that the country's longstanding policy of not recognising Israel was not new.
